step_directive_from_kip: At the very top of the Step 2 derivation section, state explicitly that the r ↔ χ coupling — i.e. χ = θ(r) mod L_χ where r = r₀·exp(αθ), so χ is a function of r through the spiral parameterization — is what makes the δ-function in Ψ_nl = N_lift·R_nl^(spiral)(θ(r))·Y_l^0(θ_pol)·δ(χ − θ(r) mod L_χ) couple the r and χ variables. This means the projection integral ∫₀^L_χ dχ is NOT a trivial integral of a constant; the δ-function picks out the spiral track and the resulting projected wavefunction has a Jacobian factor from the change of variable. State this in plain prose first, then proceed to the calculation.
